records.ety: [L. rejectus, p. p. of reicere, rejicere; pref. re- re- + jacere to throw: cf. F. rejeter, formerly also spelt rejecter. See Jet a shooting forth.]
records.morph: [imp. & p. p. Rejected; p. pr. & vb. n. Rejecting.]

defs: To cast from one; to throw away; to discard.
quotes: Therefore all this exercise of hunting . . . the Utopians have rejected to their butchers.
Reject me not from among thy children.

defs: To refuse to receive or to acknowledge; to decline haughtily or harshly; to repudiate.
quotes: That golden scepter which thou didst reject.
Because thou hast rejected knowledge, I will also reject thee, that thou shalt be no priest to me.

defs: To refuse to grant; as, to reject a prayer or request.
